attempt: Transfer Ticket 37's lesson: a bounded rank candidate becomes useful only if it names the pointwise extension theorem. Aggregate evidence and density controls remain support, not proof.

bounded_result.bounded_rank_analogue: A gap selector must force exact gap 2 for arbitrarily large ranges rather than leak mass into other bounded gaps.
bounded_result.discarded_route: bounded-gap or averaged pair mass treated as twin-prime infinitude
bounded_result.retained_route: exact gap-2 lower bound with leakage control against all wider admissible gaps

obstruction: The remaining obstruction is pointwise. Sparse exceptional objects can survive every bounded or averaged test unless a rank or positivity theorem excludes each one.
candidate_theorem: For arbitrarily large X, an unconditional nonnegative weight leaves positive mass on exact gap 2 after all wider-gap leakage is subtracted.
next_experiment: Stress candidate sieve weights against models that preserve bounded gaps while deleting exact gap-2 pairs.
claim_boundary: No twin-prime proof and no certified twin-prime counterexample.
